message:
fix: properly reap child bash processes to prevent zombie state

The bash tool used a bounded sync_channel(128) for piping child process
stdout/stderr through pump_stream reader threads. On macOS (and other
platforms with small pipe buffers), this caused a circular deadlock:

1. The bounded channel fills up (128 chunks)
2. pump_stream threads block on send(), stop reading from OS pipes
3. OS pipe buffers fill up (as small as 4KB on macOS)
4. Child process blocks on write() to stdout/stderr
5. try_wait_child() keeps returning Ok(None) — child can't exit
6. The child appears as <defunct> in pstree; UI shows "Running Bash.."

Three changes fix this:

- Replace bounded sync_channel(128) with unbounded mpsc::channel() so
  pump_stream threads never block on send() and always drain the OS
  pipe buffers, preventing the child from blocking on write().

- After the main loop exits, wait for pump_stream threads to finish
  (with a 5-second safety cap) while continuously draining the channel,
  ensuring all pipe data is captured before building the result.

- Explicitly call guard.wait() after draining to ensure the child
  process is reaped even if try_wait_child() missed it (belt-and-
  suspenders against macOS process-group edge cases).

Closes #41

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
